title: NOC 12012 – Supervisors, Library, Correspondence and Related Information Workers | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors working in libraries, correspondence units, survey departments, and regulatory documentation offices are essential to the smooth flow of information services across Canada. Under NOC 12012, these professionals enjoy strong labour-market demand and realistic pathways to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR) through skilled worker immigration programs.

classification.teer: 2 – Occupations usually requiring a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years; or supervisory occupations

duties: Coordinate clerks conducting surveys, compiling statistics, or issuing regulatory documents
Oversee library clerks responsible for shelving and cataloguing materials
Monitor service delivery and submit performance reports
Train staff in policies and work methods
Order supplies and ensure proper equipment operation
employment_requirements: College courses related to the supervised area (often required)
Several years of experience in the clerical occupation being supervised
job_outlook: From 2022 to 2031, Canada is expected to generate 48,500 new positions for Supervisors, Library, Correspondence and Related Information Workers, compared with 41,700 projected job seekers—creating favourable conditions for skilled newcomers.
additional_information: Supervisors in information and correspondence services are actively recruited in both public institutions and private organizations.

Growing hiring needs across government agencies and corporate officesStable supervisory-level rolesEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Strong prospects for employer-supported PR applicationsSalaries commonly ranging from $40,500 to $98,700 per year
Why Choose Canada for This Occupation?

Universal healthcare coverageStructured, professional workplacesExcellent work-life balanceMulticultural communitiesClear immigration routes from work permit to PRProvincial programs supporting administrative supervisors
